title: HTTP Services, Clients, Boundaries, Security, and Graceful Shutdown · Language Lens: Service Validation Security
summary: A small service accepts hostile input, calls a slow dependency, serves concurrent requests, and must shut down without losing work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go service validation security increment in a resilient secure HTTP service design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate untrusted input, escape output for its sink, minimize authority and data, protect secrets, and set explicit server resource limits.
Reuse configured clients and transports, set request contexts and timeouts, close bodies, bound reads, and classify status separately from transport errors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — Service request 5 has a hostile label, oversized body, slow dependency, duplicate header, and client cancellation during shutdown. Bound resources, validate and encode by sink, preserve stable responses, and drain owned work without leaking internals.
